summary: Add batchSize to MongoIterable based Observers
Add support for sealed traits
description: PR: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-scala-driver/pull/49

summary: Test MongoDB 4.2 in Evergreen
description: See DRIVERS-665 for details.

summary: ClassCastException while trying to decode a case class with a parameter of type scala.immutable.Set
description:
Executing find() on a MongoCollection[ModelDocument] results in ClassCastException if the ModelDocument contains a parameter of Set datatype (that also seems to extend to a scala.immutable.Map, and not necessarily for just the immutable types)

Please find the test file attached, to reproduce the error (Changes needed w.r.t specifying the MongoDb host and database/collection names.

It seems the decode() method in MacroCodec.scala  is unable to create an instance of the Set class, since it defaults to a List datatype while reading from the database.

The only other resource I found w.r.t this error was https://stackoverflow.com/questions/45860327/how-can-i-save-set-with-mongo-scala-driver.

This is reproduce-able using the mongo-scala-driver version 2.1.0, haven't tried with older versions on my side.

summary: @BsonProperty is ignored in ADT case classes
Remove deprecated API elements
UninitializedFieldError in macro generated code
description:
When using the `-Xcheckinit` scalac option, instantiating a codec generated by `Macros.createCodecProvider[T]` throws a UninitializedFieldError exception since `this` is passed down from the constructor of `MacroCodecs` into the `{{MapOfCodecsProvider}}` constructor which calls `getCodecClass` on the class which is not yet fully initialized.

 

[https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-scala-driver/pull/60] includes a possible fix for this issue.

summary: Refine GridFS binary stream interchange in GridFS
description: Consume and produce Observable[ByteBuffer]

summary: Revert Handle NPE caused by SingleResultCallback based Observables
description: SCALA-555

summary: Deprecate org.mongo.scala.bson.codecs.DEFAULT_CODEC_REGISTRY
Allow implicits to be overridden.
description: Pass by name rather than value.  See: JAVA-3578

summary: GridFSDownloadObservable can't handle > 2 GB
description: See: JAVA-3650

summary: GridFSBucket.uploadFromObservable does not complete when more the one element given in stream
description: See: JAVARS-224

summary: mongo-scala-bson has BuildInfo generated in default buildinfo package
description:
mongo-scala-bson sbt project has BuildInfoPlugin enabled

[https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-scala-driver/blob/master/project/MongoScalaBuild.scala#L180]

but no settings applied, so it uses defaults and there is generated "buildinfo.BuildInfo" object.

That can cause conflicts with other scala libraries or apps which use the same plugin, but also do not set buildinfo package. If there is another BuildInfo with other options it can potentially cause missing method fatal error.

*Note*

**There is also mongo-scala-driver sbt project which uses BuildInfoPlugin and it sets package correctly with buildInfoSettings

[https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-scala-driver/blob/master/project/MongoScalaBuild.scala#L158]

[https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-scala-driver/blob/master/project/MongoScalaBuild.scala#L66]

since driver depends on bson and as I understand the whole project uses single versioning, it would be enough to apply buildInfoSettings in bson and disable plugin in driver.

summary: GridFS upload - stack overflow
Macros.createCodecIgnoreNone() issues "DEFAULT_CODEC_REGISTRY in package codecs is deprecated" warning
description:
Mongo Scala Driver started to deprecate "org.mongodb.scala.bson.DEFAULT_CODEC_REGISTRY" from 2.9.0.

However, CaseClassCodec.createCodecDefaultCodecRegistry(), which is used by Macros.createCodecIgnoreNone(), uses the DEFAULT_CODEC_REGISTRY.
 [https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-scala-driver/blob/9b2c6d315d9e57845139fd4604ddc046c9b72a8f/bson/src/main/scala/org/mongodb/scala/bson/codecs/macrocodecs/CaseClassCodec.scala#L52]

Therefore, when I use Macros.createCodecIgnoreNone(), it generates the deprecation warning.

summary: Implement BsonEncoder
description: Implement support for using {{Encodable}} structs/classes with our BSON library

summary: make Documents Equatable
Nil out pointer to bson_t when deinitializing a document
Display error message when an error is thrown
description: While we have string messages stored with several of the cases in  `enum MongoError`, these messages are not actually printed out when an error is thrown. We should figure out how to have errors with custom messages displayed.

summary: Add documentation for Document class
Implement Database.drop
description: This was accidentally omitted from the initial API sketch, but is needed for the alpha.

summary: Audit calls to UnsafeMutablePointer.allocate to make sure deallocate is always called after
description: As stated [here|https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/unsafemutablepointer/2295026-allocate] we should always call "deallocate" when we're done with a pointer where we called allocate.

summary: Remove vendored libmongoc, link to system library
description: To aid in development, we have been compiling in a vendored copy of libmongoc and libbson. We should switch to linking dynamically against provided dylibs for these.

summary: Switch to Nimble for tests
Make Documents mutable/immutable depending on whether created with let/var
description: Currently, one can define a `Document` with `let` and then later on mutate it. By adding the `mutating` keyword to the subscript setter, we can make it more clear what is happening. This will likely require updating a number of places in the code where we rely on the current behavior.

summary: Add README to repository
Add "name" property to collection and database
description: We have easy access to these, and they may be useful to users.

summary: Convert benchmark results into a form that can be used for graphing on Evergreen
Change tests that rely on JSON files to work in Xcode, and document tests in README
description:
As we discovered from a [recent issue|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/issues/48], it's not obvious that a mongod needs to be running for most of our tests. Additionally, the tests that rely on parsing JSON files don't work in Xcode. 

We should 
a) Add a section in the README about our tests
b) Fix the tests that use files so they can run in Xcode

summary: Make internal OpaquePointers to libmongoc types optional
description: In MongoClient, MongoDatabase, MongoCollection, MongoCursor, we initialize internal pointers to OpaquePointer(bitPattern: 1). In cases where initialization fails, this can lead to attempting to free the address 0x0000000000000001. We should instead make the pointers optionals with no initial value, so we don't try to free them if they aren't set to real addresses.

summary: Implement a custom RegularExpression struct
description:
This will allow us to make it Codable. Currently, we use an NSRegularExpression, which is not Codable and cannot be made to conform in an extension.

It should have a computed property that returns itself as an NSRegularExpression.

summary: Implement BSON Decoder
description: Implement support for using {{Decodable}} structs/classes with our BSON library

summary: support strict decoding of Document to a codable struct
Support JSON fallback in BSONDecoder.decode from Data
description:
Currently {{BSONDecoder}}s method for decoding Data assumes strict bson (expecting a wrapping object). It's possible for stitch to have bare JSON values, which are permitted in Extended JSON. We should add a new convenience method to {{BSONDecoder}} that: 
  - takes in JSON/extended JSON
  - attempts to create a {{Document}} from that data
  - if parsing into {{Document}} fails, fall back to using {{JSONDecoder}}

summary: Rewrite BsonEncoder to conform to Encoder protocol
description: We have a working BsonEncoder, but it will be more powerful if it conforms to the Encoder protocol and can work with all Swift Encodable types.

summary: Convert all options types to use the new BsonEncoder
Sort out Swift 4.0 vs Swift 4.1 issues
description:
As [~jason.flax] encountered, BsonDecoder is using compactMap in some copy-pasted code from the JSONDecoder, which is new in Swift 4.1. This was also pointed out by someone in a [GitHub issue|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/issues/60] who wants to test the driver with IBM Z which only supports up to Swift 4.0. 

I have been sloppy about making sure I only use 4.0 stuff because I had 4.1 installed and didn't bother switching back, but I found a nice tool [swiftenv|https://swiftenv.fuller.li/en/latest/] to let us switch versions easily for the future. 

It seems like we probably want to keep supporting Swift 4.0 so we should resolve these issues. And standardize what version(s) we are testing (maybe we should set up travis or something?)

summary: Support encoding arrays at the top level
description: One should be able to encode an `[Codable]` and get back an `[Document]`

summary: Support building the driver on Linux
description:
We need a LinuxMain.swift file for the tests to run. Also, apparently Notification.Name requires an argument label - see this [issue|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/issues/60]. 

Apparently once those issues are fixed, there is a segfault. 

We should resolve the issues that prevent the driver from building and then investigate the source of the segfault.

summary: upsertedId should be a `BsonValue?`, not `Any`
description: As pointed out by [~adam.chelminski@mongodb.com], UpdateResult.upsertedId is Any, but should really be a `BsonValue?`, since it will always be some valid BSON type if it exists. I believe this was a carry over from the CRUD spec where "Any" is used to mean "any BSON type".

summary: Refactor how ReadConcerns are encoded to options types
description: We've determined a better solution that involves making ReadConcern encodable and modifying the options before encoding to remove read concerns that the spec requires we omit. We should implement this, and then follow the same pattern for encoding options that support WriteConcern

summary: mongoc_init and mongoc_cleanup are never called
description:
We are presently not ever calling these functions, which the documentation for mongoc explicit states as a requirement.

The CXX driver requires the user to initialize a `mongocxx::instance` at the start of a program, which calls `libmongoc::init()`; the destructor for a  `mongocxx::instance` then calls `libmongoc::cleanup()`. 

We should implement this similarly, and maybe reference-count the mongoc instance in `MongoClient.deinit` to determine whether any other clients exist that point to it, and if not call cleanup.

summary: Move ReadConcernLevel type into ReadConcern
description: rather than having it as a separate type, let's move it into ReadConcern and rename it just Level so its full name is ReadConcern.Level.

summary: BSON Document should allow UnsafePointer where possible
description: BSON Document currently requires an UnsafeMutablePointer in various places where it is only reading BSON data. For flexibility, we accept an UnsafePointer.

summary: Send client info indicating we are in Swift driver
description: Right now it says we are using libmongoc. we should use mongoc_handshake_data_append to say we are using the swift driver

summary: Add support for "hint" and "CursorType" options to Collection API functions
description: We skipped these in the original implementation, but `find` supports a `cursorType` option and aggregate/count/find all support a `hint`.

summary: Support encoding/decoding untyped BsonValues
description: We should support encoding and decoding things that are only known to be BsonValues, since there are a limited number of BsonValue types and they are all Codable.

summary: Update MongoMobile to use new ABI v1 of MongoEmbedded
description: The new ABI has been released, and we need to update our builds accordingly

summary: Enable deleteOne-collation and deleteMany-collation crud tests
description: We were skipping some of the CRUD tests (those for deleteOne and deleteMany with collation) because of a bug in the C driver (CDRIVER-2527). This was fixed in v1.10 so we should update the test runner to run them now.
